title: Do any 4-regular vertex-transitive Ramanujan graphs on 48 or 52 vertices fail global rigidity?
micro_paper_assessment: A real theorem slice is now visible, but the exact 48/52 residue is still open because the surviving tiny-island obstruction cases have not been certified against actual candidate graphs.
family_affinity: High; this partial result sits directly on the finite-residue threshold-sharpening lane from the 2023 theorem.
generalization_signal: The quotient-template interlacing method should transfer to other finite Ramanujan rigidity residues, but the final tiny-island cleanup is family-specific.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any 4-regular Ramanujan graph on 48 or 52 vertices is 3-connected; moreover, every 3-vertex cut isolates a component on at most 5 vertices, and every nontrivial essential edge cut isolates a side on between 4 and 9 vertices.
next_action: Obtain or reconstruct the actual 48- and 52-vertex candidate graphs, then certify that none realizes the surviving 3-cut islands of size 2..5 or essential 4-edge-cut islands of size 4..9.
